message:
cleanup(mcp): remove obsolete tick_budget_exceeded local + add queries-side drop_close event capture tests

Two small follow-ups.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs
- The previous ATC consolidation commit routed both `tick_budget_exceeded`
  and `note` through `atc_operator_tick_budget_status`, but left the
  older stand-alone `let tick_budget_exceeded = tick_duration_micros
  > tick_budget_micros;` line above the helper call. It was shadowed
  immediately by the helper's pair destructure; drop the dead line
  now that it serves no purpose.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs
- Port the `EventCapture` + `CapturedEvent` + `EventFieldCapture`
  tracing subscriber harness from `mailbox_verdict.rs` into the
  queries-side test module. The harness's `drop_close_count()`
  helper returns the number of `fsqlite::runtime` events whose
  `event` field contains `"drop_close"`, so queries-side tests can
  assert regressions in the DbConnGuard wrapping at the point where
  the ATC rollup / snapshot / restore helpers take out a
  connection. Uses the same `Arc<(AtomicU64 span counter)>` pattern
  for Send+Sync safety alongside concurrent query tests.

Both parts are test-infrastructure / dead-code-cleanup only; no
runtime behaviour change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
